Comment GrimbaNews classe-t-il le biais: How does GrimbaNews classify a source's bias?
Nous combinons quatre signaux : AllSides: We combine four signals: AllSides (reader panel with blind survey), Ad Fontes Media (linguistic analysis + review), Media Bias / Fact Check (independent editorial review), and our own editorial board for Francophone and African sources outside the coverage of the three English-language agencies. The result is a seven-level score: Far left, Left, Center-left, Center, Center-right, Right, Far right.
Comment fonctionne le baromètre de couve: How does the coverage barometer work?
Sur chaque dossier, nous comptons les so: For each dossier, we count the sources covering it and compute each camp's share (left / center / right). The barometer shows that split as three segments. The more one side dominates, the more likely it is that the other side has a blindspot on the topic.
Un angle mort est une histoire important: A blindspot is an important story reported almost exclusively by one political camp. If nearly all sources covering a topic lean right, readers who only read the left don't know the topic exists — that's their blindspot. Our /angles-morts page lists these stories with two tabs: "For the left" and "For the right".
Pourquoi gardez-vous le bleu pour la gau: Why do you keep blue for the left and red for the right?
C'est la convention francophone et conti: That's the Francophone and continental convention. In the United States the convention is reversed — that's what creates the confusion on Anglo tools. We chose one stable color code, Francophone by default, for every edition of the site, and we document it on the "Understanding the barometer" page.
Est-ce que le score de fiabilité est le: Is the reliability score the same as bias?
Non. Une source peut être très fiable et: No. A source can be very reliable and have a strong bias — for example a source with high editorial quality but clearly on the left. Reliability measures sourcing quality, correction speed, and respect for context. Bias measures political orientation. We display the two separately so readers can make their own call.
Comment contester un classement ?: How can I challenge a classification?
Chaque page de source a un lien « Contes: Each source page has a "Challenge a classification" link that opens our contact form. We publish every receivable challenge and its status on an open audit page (under construction).
NobuAI est notre assistant éditorial. Il: NobuAI is our editorial assistant. It helps generate multi-source summaries and framing comparisons. It is always assisted by a human editorial board that validates before publication — no content ships without human review.
Non. Les synthèses sont extractives : No: No. Summaries are extractive: NobuAI condenses sentences and paragraphs already present in the source articles. No fact is synthesized without a citable source. If you see a claim in a summary that doesn't seem attributable to a source, flag it — we correct publicly.
Avez-vous besoin que je crée un compte ?: Do you need me to create an account?
Non, pas pour la lecture. Vos préférence: No, not for reading. Your preferences (followed topics, saved articles, language, theme, edition) live in browser cookies. You can clear your cookies to erase everything.
Que faites-vous avec mes données ?: What do you do with my data?
Pas grand-chose. Pas de tracker tiers. P: Not much. No third-party tracker. No marketing cookie. No sharing. For audience metrics we use anonymous server-side counters. The detail is in our privacy policy.
Comment résilier la newsletter ?: How do I unsubscribe from the newsletter?
Chaque email contient un lien de désabon: Each email contains an unsubscribe link. The click takes effect immediately, with no extra confirmation email.
La lecture du site est gratuite — homepa: The site is free to read — homepage, source list, dossiers, blindspots, /coffre. The paid tier unlocks the full article text (instead of the excerpt + external link), removes ad slots, and unlocks the daily and thematic newsletters. Blindspots are NEVER paywalled.
Comment soutenir le projet sans payer un: How can I support the project without paying for a subscription?
Partagez les articles que vous lisez ici: Share the articles you read here. Challenge our classifications when you think we got it wrong. Suggest Francophone and African sources we should follow. The project lives as much on editorial feedback as on revenue.

Autres angles sur la même histoire: Other angles on the same story
GrimbaNews est un média francophone indé: GrimbaNews is an independent Francophone newsroom published by Iboga Ventures. Our goal: to let our readers see every angle of every story. We don't claim neutrality — we make it visible, quantifiable, and challengeable.
<strong>Transparence</strong> — chaque c: Transparency — every classification is public; every source has a profile showing its score, orientation, and ownership type.
<strong>Pluralisme</strong> — les angles: Pluralism — left, center, and right angles are always represented when they exist. When one is missing, we flag it (blindspot).
<strong>Révision continue</strong> — nos: Continuous review — our classifications evolve. An outlet can move up or down the grid based on its practices.
Chaque source suivie est placée sur un a: Every tracked source is placed on a simple three-position axis:
<strong>Choix éditorial des sujets</stro: Editorial topic choice — which stories are covered, which are missing from the feed, over a rolling thirty-day window.
<strong>Cadrage linguistique</strong> —: Linguistic framing — lexical analysis of headlines and standfirsts (vocabulary, connotations, semantic field), compared to a reference corpus.
<strong>Structure de l'argumentation</st: Argument structure — in editorials and op-eds, ratio between sources cited on the left, center, and right.
<strong>Validation croisée externe</stro: External cross-validation — our classifications are calibrated against the AllSides and Media Bias/Fact Check methodologies, adapted to the Francophone media landscape.
Un classement n'est <em>jamais</em> un j: A classification is never a judgment about an article's factual accuracy. A left-leaning source can be very credible; a center source can be low-credibility. They are two distinct dimensions.
Un <strong>angle mort</strong> est une h: A blindspot is an important story that only one side of the political spectrum covers. For example, a topic whose 95% of articles come from left-classified sources is flagged as a left blindspot. This is not a judgment about the truth of the story, just an editorial signal: this story exists, and your usual feed may not cover it.
GrimbaNews calcule chaque jour les angle: GrimbaNews computes blindspots daily from the story clusters our engine groups. A cluster is flagged as a blindspot when coverage exceeds 80% from a single camp across at least six distinct sources.
Le score de crédibilité combine quatre i: The credibility score combines four indicators, each rated out of 25:
<strong>Fact-checking</strong> — taux d': Fact-checking — rate of corrected or retracted articles over the last twelve months.
<strong>Sourcing</strong> — part d'artic: Sourcing — share of articles with explicit sources (names, documents, data).
<strong>Séparation info / opinion</stron: News / opinion separation — clear distinction between wire copy, investigations, analyses, and editorials.
<strong>Historique éditorial</strong> —: Editorial track record — line consistency, longevity, journalistic awards.
Un score ≥ 85 est affiché en <span style: A score ≥ 85 is shown in green; 70 to 84 in amber; below 70 in red. The score doesn't replace the reader's judgment — it informs it.
Savoir qui possède un média éclaire ce q: Knowing who owns an outlet illuminates what we read. We classify every source into four categories:
<strong>Indépendant</strong> — capital d: Independent — capital held by the newsroom or by shareholders without significant other economic activity.
<strong>Privé</strong> — détenu par un g: Private — owned by an industrial or financial group ("interest-driven" press).
<strong>État</strong> — financé par l'ar: State — funded by public money; newsroom autonomy varies by country.
Vous pensez qu'une source est mal classé: Think a source is mis-classified? You're right to ask. Write to us at methodologie@grimbanews.com with your argument and concrete examples (headlines, dates, screenshots). Every challenge is reviewed by our editorial board within fifteen days and the response is public.
Le <a href=\":url\">journal des révision: The revision log tracks every classification change with its date and motivation.
GrimbaNews is published by Iboga Ventures. We accept no advertising funding from political parties, governments, or press groups whose bias we classify. Our revenue comes exclusively from subscriptions and non-profit sponsorship.
La direction éditoriale est indépendante: The editorial leadership is independent of the executive leadership. No content can be pulled at a shareholder's request.
Cette méthodologie évolue. Les mises à j: This methodology evolves. Updates are dated and archived. Your feedback helps us improve it.
Chaque source affiche jusqu'à trois étiq: Every source displays up to three labels: its editorial bias on 7 levels, its reliability on 5 levels, its ownership type on 8 categories. Here is what they look like and what they mean.
Moyenne pondérée des quatre signaux (All: Weighted average of the four signals (AllSides + Ad Fontes + MBFC + GrimbaNews board). The coverage barometer on each dossier compresses these 7 levels into 3 camps (left / center / right) to stay legible.
Combinaison de la qualité du sourcing, d: Combines sourcing quality, correction speed, language sensationalism, and respect for context.
Catégorie d'actionnaire principal. Mise: Primary shareholder category. Updated case-by-case by the editorial board.
La plupart des lecteurs francophones n'o: Most Francophone readers don't have access to a tool that shows, on the same story, what the left, center, and right are writing. Anglo aggregators exist but are biased by their US-centric reference grid. The Francophone press is what it is. The Francophone African press is under-represented everywhere.
GrimbaNews cible cette absence : un seul: GrimbaNews targets that absence: a single Francophone tool that aggregates what's written, labels each source's editorial bias and reliability, and clearly flags blindspots. Not another news feed — a reading frame.
Avec un focus assumé sur les éditions Af: With a deliberate focus on the Africa and International editions — because that's precisely where existing tools leave Francophone readers with the worst blindspots.
On agrège l'actualité depuis des centain: We aggregate the news from hundreds of Francophone and international sources, classified across 7 editorial-bias levels and 5 reliability levels.
On regroupe les articles couvrant le mêm: We group articles covering the same event into dossiers and display a barometer showing the left / center / right split of the sources covering it.
On signale les angles morts — les histoi: We flag blindspots — stories that only one camp covers — on a dedicated page.
On donne au lecteur les outils pour comp: We give the reader tools to compare framing: select two or three sources and see their headlines side by side on the same story.
On documente la propriété de chaque sour: We document the ownership of every source — government, conglomerate, individual, telecom, independent…
On laisse le lecteur sauvegarder, suivre: We let the reader save, follow topics, and read without registration. No personal data is required for basic use.
Tous nos classements sont publics, datés: All our classifications are public, dated, and challengeable. The methodology is documented page by page.
Nous n'acceptons pas de financement publ: We accept no advertising funding from political parties, governments, or press groups whose bias we classify. Our revenue comes exclusively from subscriptions and non-profit sponsorship.
Nos synthèses multi-sources sont assisté: Our multi-source summaries are NobuAI-assisted but reviewed by our editorial board. No summary ships without human review.
Pas de tracker tiers. Les préférences de: No third-party tracker. Reading preferences (followed topics, saved articles, language, theme) live in your browser cookies, not on our servers.
